revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂積老]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|occupation={{ill|poet|qid=Q49757|1=Poet}}|father={{ill|Q22129398|qid=Q22129398|1=Hozumi Mushimaro}}}}
{{Infobox noble | name = Hozumi no Oyu (穂積老) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Late Asuka period]] - [[Mid Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = [[:en:Tenpyō Shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 1, {{ill|August 26 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月26日 (旧暦)|lt=26|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 26 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} ({{ill|October 11|en|October 11|qid=Q2927|lt=October 11|1=October 11}}, {{ill|749|en|749|qid=Q23624|lt=749|1=749}}) | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] [[Ōkura no Taifu]] | lord = {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} → [[Emperor Shōmu]] | clan = [[Hozumi clan|Hozumi]] [[Asomi|no Asomi]] | parents = | children = [[Hozumi no Noomimaro|Noomimaro]]?<ref name="Ota" /> | notable_positions = }}
'''Hozumi no Oyu''' (穂積老) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|Japanese nobility]] and {{ill|Poet|en|Poet|qid=Q49757|lt=Poet|1=Poet}} during the [[Late Asuka period]] to [[Mid Nara period]]. He was a [[:en:court official|court official]] and {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yō poet|1=Man'yōshū}}. His court Rank was [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]], and he served as [[Ōkura no Taifu]].
== Career == In the [[Daihō]] era (3rd year, {{ill|703|en|703|qid=Q23500|lt=703|1=703}}), Oyu served as a [[Sanyōdō Circuit|Sanyō Circuit]] {{ill|inspector|en|inspector|qid=Q511138|lt=inspector|1=Inspector}} (巡察使) while holding the Rank of [[Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade]].
In the [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] era (2nd year, {{ill|709|en|709|qid=Q23514|lt=709|1=709}}), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, skipping four Ranks. In the following year, during the {{ill|New Year's|en|New Year's|qid=Q109943356|lt=New Year's|1=New Year's}} court ceremony under {{ill|Empress Genmei|en|Empress Genmei|qid=Q233224|lt=Empress Genmei|1=Empress Genmei}}, he participated as a sub-general under [[Ōtomo no Tabito]], leading horsemen, {{ill|Hayato|en|Hayato|qid=Q8184907|lt=Hayato|1=Hayato}}, and {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|qid=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}} warriors along the [[Suzaku Avenue]] parade.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi'', Wadō 3rd year, 1st month, 1st day entry.</ref> He was subsequently promoted to [[:en:Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] in [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 6 (713), [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] in {{ill|Reiki|en|Reiki|qid=Q183057|lt=Reiki|1=Reiki}} 3 (717), and [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] with an appointment as [[Shikibu Daifu]] ([[Assistant Minister of Ceremonial Affairs]]) in {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 2 (718). During this period, he delivered the elegy for [[Ishikami no Maro]], the [[:en:Sadaijin|Sadaijin]] (Minister of the Left), representing officials of Fifth Rank or higher.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Reiki 3rd year, 3rd month, 3rd day entry.</ref>
In {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 6 (722), Oyu criticized {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}, leading to charges of [[:en:disrespect|disrespect]]. Initially sentenced to {{ill|Decapitation|en|Decapitation|qid=Q204933|lt=Decapitation|1=Decapitation}}, his sentence was reduced to {{ill|Exile|en|Exile|qid=Q188863|lt=Exile|1=Exile}} on [[Sado Island]] following the intercession of Crown Prince [[Shōmu]] (later Emperor Shōmu).<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Yōrō 6th year, 1st month, 20th day entry.</ref>
He was pardoned in {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 12 (740) during Emperor Shōmu's general {{ill|Amnesty|en|Amnesty|qid=Q185943|lt=Amnesty|1=Amnesty}} and returned to the capital. Later, he was restored to his former Rank and appointed as [[Ōkura no Taifu]]. In {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 16 (744), he served as a [[Guardian Official]] (留守官) at {{ill|Kuni-kyō|en|Kuni-kyō|qid=Q1155335|lt=Kuni-kyō|1=Kuni-kyō}} during the court's relocation to {{ill|Naniwa-kyō|en|Naniwa-kyō|qid=Q1042338|lt=Naniwa-kyō|1=Naniwa-kyō}}.<ref>''Shoku Nihongi,'' Tenpyō 16th year, 2nd month, 2nd day entry.</ref> In [[:en:Tenpyō Shōhō|Tenpyō Shōhō]] 1 (749), Oyu passed away on the 26th day of the 8th month. His final Rank and position were [[Ōkura no Taifu]] and [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]].
== Poetry == The ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|qid=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'' includes a {{ill|Tanka|en|Tanka|qid=Q258331|lt=Tanka|1=Tanka}} composed during an [[imperial procession]] to [[:en:Shiga|Shiga]] in {{ill|Ōtsu|en|Ōtsu|qid=Q202907|lt=Ōtsu|1=Ōtsu}}<ref>''Man'yōshū,'' Vol. 3, Poem 288</ref> and a [[:en:Chōka|Chōka]] and {{ill|envoy|en|envoy|qid=Q5381413|lt=envoy|1=Envoy}} written during his exile on [[Sado Island]].<ref>''Man'yōshū,'' Vol. 13, Poems 3240, 3241 (Commentary from ''Man'yōshū Kogi'' by Kashimo Masasumi).</ref>
''Man'yōshū,'' Vol. 3, Poem 288: {{quotation|If my life persists Fortune may bring me again To see Shiga’s shores, To the waves meeting the land, At Otsu, where they arise.}}
''Man'yōshū,'' Vol. 13, Poems 3240, 3241: {{quotation|In awe of the great Sovereign’s command, I cross Nara’s lofty hills, Navigate swift torrents, and Traverse rivers with vigor, Taming the wild tides, And passing through Ōmi, A lover's farewell marks The sacred crossroads Of Sasanami's shoreline, Eternal longing endures.}}
Envoy: {{quotation|I pray for blessings, To once again see Shiga, At Tangasaki.}}
== Official Record == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * Date unknown: [[Senior Eighth Rank, Upper Grade]] * [[Daihō]] 3 ({{ill|703|en|703|qid=Q23500|lt=703|1=703}}): Inspector of the [[Sanyō Circuit]] * Date unknown: [[Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade]] * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 2 ({{ill|709|en|709|qid=Q23514|lt=709|1=709}}): Promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} * Wadō 6 ({{ill|713|en|713|qid=Q23523|lt=713|1=713}}): Promoted to [[:en:Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] * {{ill|Reiki|en|Reiki|qid=Q183057|lt=Reiki|1=Reiki}} 3 ({{ill|717|en|717|qid=Q23532|lt=717|1=717}}): Promoted to [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] * {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|718|en|718|qid=Q23534|lt=718|1=718}}): Promoted to [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; appointed [[Shikibu Daifu]] * Yōrō 6 ({{ill|722|en|722|qid=Q23542|lt=722|1=722}}): Exiled to [[Sado Island]] * {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 12 ({{ill|740|en|740|qid=Q23593|lt=740|1=740}}): Pardoned and returned to the capital * Date unknown: Reinstated [[:en:Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]] * Tenpyō 16 ({{ill|744|en|744|qid=Q23608|lt=744|1=744}}): Recorded as [[Ōkura no Taifu]]
== Genealogy == There is no direct evidence of Oyu's descendants. However, [[Hozumi no Noomimaro]] is sometimes postulated as his son.<ref name="Ota">Hozumi and Mononobe clan genealogies, as recorded in [[Ota Ryo]]’s ''Seishi Kakei Daijiten''.</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Sources == * [[Mitsuo Ujitani]], ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1),'' Kodansha Academic Library, 1995 * Junsuke Suzuki, ''Hozumi-kei Suzuki-shi: Kishu Tokugawa 270-nen Suzuki Kakeifu,'' privately published, 1982. {{NDL|000001625006}} * [[Toshio Hōga]], ''Ancient Clan Genealogical Compilation,'' Ancient Clan Research Association, 1986.
{{translated page|ja|穂積老|version=99514093|comment=Imported full ja history}}
